enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Undertaking of Financial Services) Directions, 2025

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 1These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
enriched_requirements.object: immediate effect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'RCBs' and individually as 'an RCB').
enriched_requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'RCBs' and individually as 'an RCB')

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, rural co-operative banks shall mean State Co-operative Banks
and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture
and Rural Development Act, 1981.
enriched_requirements.object: and Central Co-operative Banks, as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In these directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Debtor company' means any company to which an RCB currently has or
previously had a loan or investment exposure (excluding equity instruments)
anytime during the preceding twelve months;
enriched_requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Debtor company' means any company to which an RCB currently has or previously had a loan or investment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Equity instrument' means equity shares, compulsorily convertible
preference shares (CCPS) and compulsorily convertible debentures (CCD);
enriched_requirements.object: preference shares (CCPS) and compulsorily convertible debentures (CCD)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Financial Services Company' means a company engaged in the 'business
of financial services'.
enriched_requirements.object: the 'business of financial services'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: The 'business of financial services' shall include -
1 Modified w.e.f.
enriched_requirements.object: w

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Board of an RCB shall:
(1) evaluate and approve any proposal to enter into a long-term agreement for
undertaking insurance business on a referral basis, upon completion of the
initial contract period,
(2) approve implementation of customer grievance redressal mechanism for
merchant acquisition business,
(3) approve a policy on merchant acquisition for card transactions,
(4) evaluate and approve a resolution for undertaking insurance business as
a corporate agent without risk participation,
(5) evaluate and approve resolution for undertaking the business of Pre-paid
Payment Instruments (PPIs).
enriched_requirements.object: approve any proposal to enter into a long-term agreement for undertaking insurance business on a referral basis, upon completion of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, it shall have satisfactorily
implemented a comprehensive Board approved policy on Customer
grievance redressal mechanism which includes escalation matrix for
resolution of customer complaints.
enriched_requirements.object: comprehensive Board approved policy on Customer grievance redressal mechanism which includes escalation matrix for r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB's investment policy shall have suitable provisions governing its
investments in an AIF Scheme, compliant with extant law and regulations.
enriched_requirements.object: its investments in an AIF Scheme, compliant with extant law and regulations

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not individually contribute more than 10 percent of the corpus of
an AIF Scheme.
enriched_requirements.object: 10 percent of the corpus of an AIF Scheme

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate contribution by all Regulated Entities (REs) in any AIF Scheme
shall not be more than 20 percent of the corpus of that scheme.
enriched_requirements.object: percent of the corpus of that scheme

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'RE' shall mean:
(1) Commercial Banks (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks);
enriched_requirements.object: (including Small Finance Banks, Local Area Banks)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Where an RCB contributes more than five percent of the corpus of an AIF
Scheme that has downstream investment (excluding equity instruments) in a
debtor company of the RCB, the RCB shall be required to make 100 percent
provision to the extent of its proportionate investment in the debtor company
through the AIF Scheme, subject to a cap equivalent to RCB's direct loan and /
or investment exposure to the said debtor company.
enriched_requirements.object: 100 percent provision to the extent of its proportionate investment in the debtor company through the AIF Scheme

conditions:
where an RCB's contribution is
in the form of subordinated units
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph 10, where an RCB's contribution is
in the form of subordinated units, it shall deduct the entire investment from its
capital funds - proportionately from both Tier-1 and Tier-2 capital (wherever
applicable).
enriched_requirements.object: from its capital funds - proportionately from both Tier-1 and Tier-2 capital (wherever applicable)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
2The RBI may, in consultation with the Government of India, by way of a
notification, exempt certain AIFs (Annex I) from these Directions on AIF
contained in Part B of Chapter II, except for paragraph 7.
enriched_requirements.object: the Government of India, by way of a notification, exempt certain AIFs (Annex I) from these Directions on AIF contained in Part B of Chapter II

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall be permitted to undertake insurance business as corporate agent
without risk participation, subject to it fulfilling the following terms and conditions:
(1) An RCB shall be a Scheduled or licensed State Co-operative Bank (SCB)
or a licensed District Central Co-operative Bank (DCCB);
enriched_requirements.object: insurance business as corporate agent without risk participation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An RCB shall have a minimum positive net worth [real or exchangeable
value of paid-up capital and reserves as defined in Section 11 of the
Banking Regulation Act, 1949(AACS)] of ₹50 crore as per the latest
NABARD Inspection Report;
enriched_requirements.object: net worth [real or exchangeable value of paid-up capital and reserves as defined in Section 11 of the Banking Regulation Act,

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An RCB shall have earned net profit for the last three years and shall not
have any accumulated losses;
(4) The Gross NPA of the RCB shall not exceed 10 percent;

(5) An RCB shall not have violated prudential norms including individual and
group exposure norms;
enriched_requirements.object: including individual and group exposure norms

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) An RCB shall have complied with the guidelines on loans and advances to
directors, their relatives and associated firms;
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines on loans and advances to directors, their relatives and associated firms

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Premium collection accounts shall not be opened with the RCB and
premium collected shall be directly paid to the insurance companies;
enriched_requirements.object: RCB and premium collected shall be directly paid to the insurance companies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (8) An RCB shall adhere to IRDAI regulations prescribed for corporate agents;
enriched_requirements.object: prescribed for corporate agents
the effect that banking business will not in any way get contaminated/affected on account of acting as agent of insurance com

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
(10) An RCB shall not adopt any restrictive practice of compelling customers to
opt for a specific insurance company, particularly in respect of assets
financed by the RCB.
enriched_requirements.object: of compelling customers to opt for a specific insurance company, particularly in respect of assets financed by the RCB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Customers shall be free to exercise their own choice
of insurer.
enriched_requirements.object: their own choice of insurer

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB fulfilling the above conditions and desirous of undertaking insurance
business as corporate agent without risk participation, shall apply through
Pravaah Portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) to Department of Regulation, RBI with
requisite information and documents.
enriched_requirements.object: (https://pravaah

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The application shall be accompanied by:
(1) A certified copy of the Board Resolution approving the proposal;
enriched_requirements.object: A certified copy of the Board Resolution approving the proposal

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not undertake insurance business without obtaining prior
permission of the RBI.
enriched_requirements.object: obtaining prior permission of the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The permission granted to RCB to undertake insurance business on non-risk
participation basis shall be subject to their obtaining necessary authorisation/
license from IRDAI.
enriched_requirements.object: obtaining necessary authorisation/ license from IRDAI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The said permission shall not be construed as a permission to adjust the dues
of the insured from out of the proceeds of insurance claims.
enriched_requirements.object: permission to adjust the dues of the insured from out of the proceeds of insurance claims

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Any adjustment of dues from insurance claims shall be governed strictly under
a tri-party agreement among the insured, the insurer, and the insurance agent.
enriched_requirements.object: a tri-party agreement among the insured, the insurer, and the insurance agent

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The permission granted to an RCB for undertaking insurance agency business
shall be valid for a period of two years, subject to review prior to its expiry.
enriched_requirements.object: period of two years

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall obtain renewal of permission by applying through Pravaah Portal
(https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) to Department of Regulation, RBI, provided the RCB
continues to comply with the stipulated terms and conditions.
enriched_requirements.object: by applying through Pravaah Portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) to Department of Regulation, RBI

conditions:
In case of non-compliance with any of the conditions at any time after the grant
of permission
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of non-compliance with any of the conditions at any time after the grant
of permission, such permission shall be liable to be withdrawn.
enriched_requirements.object: withdrawn

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Conditions for Undertaking Referral Business: Under the referral arrangement,
an RCB shall provide physical infrastructure within its branch premises to
insurance companies for selling insurance products to the RCB's customers with
adequate disclosure and transparency, and in turn earn referral fees on the basis
of premia collected.
enriched_requirements.object: its branch premises to insurance companies for selling insurance products to the RCB's customers with adequate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The above permission shall be subject to the following
conditions:
(1) An RCB shall comply with the relevant IRDAI regulations applicable to
referral arrangements with insurance companies.
enriched_requirements.object: following conditions: (1) An RCB shall comply with the relevant IRDAI regulations applicable to referral arrangements with insurance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) An RCB shall not adopt any restrictive practice of compelling customers to
opt for a specific insurance company, particularly in respect of assets
financed by the RCB.
enriched_requirements.object: of compelling customers to opt for a specific insurance company, particularly in respect of assets financed by the RCB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) An RCB desirous of entering into referral arrangement, in addition to
complying with IRDAI regulations, shall enter into a formal agreement with
the concerned insurance company to permit use of the RCB's premises,
and allow use of existing infrastructure.
enriched_requirements.object: agreement with the concerned insurance company to permit use of the RCB's premises, and allow use of existing infrastructure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The initial agreement shall be for a period not exceeding three (3) years,
with the RCB retaining discretion to renegotiate the terms based on service
performance, or enter into a new agreement after the initial period.
enriched_requirements.object: not exceeding three (3) years, with the RCB retaining discretion to renegotiate the terms based on service performance, or enter into

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Upon completion of the initial period, an RCB may enter into a longer-term
contract subject to Board approval.
enriched_requirements.object: contract subject to Board approval

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Participation of customers of an RCB in insurance products shall be purely
voluntary.

This must be clearly stated in all publicity material issued by the
RCB.
enriched_requirements.object: all publicity material issued by the RCB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be no direct or indirect linkage between the provision of
banking services and the availing of insurance products.
enriched_requirements.object: indirect linkage between the provision of banking services and the availing of insurance products

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Any risk associated with the referral arrangement shall not be transferred
to the business of the RCB.
enriched_requirements.object: business of the RCB

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall not require prior approval of the RBI to undertake referral
business.
enriched_requirements.object: the RBI to undertake referral business

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB not intending to act as Point of Sale (POS) acquiring bank is permitted
to deploy third party POS terminals without prior approval of RBI subject to
fulfilling the following criteria:
(1) An RCB shall be licensed by the RBI and CBS compliant;
enriched_requirements.object: RBI and CBS compliant

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The CRAR shall not be less than nine percent in the preceding financial
year;
enriched_requirements.object: percent in the preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (3) An RCB shall have made a net profit in the preceding financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: profit in the preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (4) The Board of RCB shall consist of at least two professional directors;
enriched_requirements.object: two professional directors

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) An RCB shall have a Board approved customer grievance redressal
mechanism;
enriched_requirements.object: customer grievance redressal mechanism

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) An RCB shall put in place policy approved by the Board for merchant
acquisition for card transactions;
enriched_requirements.object: approved by the Board for merchant acquisition for card transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) There shall not be any restrictions imposed on the RCB for accepting
deposits/withdrawals by the RBI;
enriched_requirements.object: on the RCB for accepting deposits/withdrawals by the RBI

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(8) An RCB shall obtain consent from its merchant customers before offering
third party POS terminals and shall disclose the settlement process;
enriched_requirements.object: merchant customers before offering third party POS terminals and shall disclose the settlement process

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(9) An RCB shall report the operationalization of third-party POS terminals to
the respective Regional Office of RBI within a month, along with the
necessary documents.
enriched_requirements.object: third-party POS terminals to the respective Regional Office of RBI within a month, along with the necessary documents

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB intending to act as POS acquiring bank shall be permitted to deploy its
own POS terminals with prior approval of RBI subject to the RCB fulfilling the
following criteria:
(1) An RCB shall comply with criteria mentioned in clauses (1) to (7) of
paragraph 28 above.
enriched_requirements.object: its own POS terminals with prior approval of RBI subject to the RCB fulfilling the following criteria: (1) An RCB shall comply

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) The RCB's IT systems and CBS shall have been subjected to an IS Audit
not earlier than six months from the date of application, certifying that the
systems are adequately secure.
enriched_requirements.object: an IS Audit not earlier than six months from the date of application, certifying that the systems are adequately secure

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The assessed net-worth of the RCB shall be more than ₹25 crore as per
the last supervisory inspection;
enriched_requirements.object: crore as per the last supervisory inspection

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The Gross NPA of the RCB shall be less than seven percent and net NPAs
shall be less than 3 percent in the preceding financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: percent and net NPAs shall be less than 3 percent in the preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) An RCB shall not have been subjected to monetary penalty during the last
two financial years and in the year of application;
enriched_requirements.object: monetary penalty during the last two financial years and in the year of application

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) There shall not be any default in the maintenance of CRR/SLR during the
preceding financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: the maintenance of CRR/SLR during the preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) An RCB shall be a member of authorized card network, such as RuPay,
Visa, or MasterCard, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: authorized card network, such as RuPay, Visa, or MasterCard, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB shall comply with instructions and guidelines on Merchant Acquisition
for card transactions and POS issued by Department of Payment and Settlement
Systems, RBI from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: guidelines on Merchant Acquisition for card transactions and POS issued by Department of Payment and Settlement Systems,

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
An RCB desirous to deploy their own POS terminals and act as POS acquiring
bank shall apply through Pravaah Portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in) to
Department of Regulation, RBI, for necessary permission, with the requisite
information and documents.
enriched_requirements.object: (https://pravaah

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A licensed RCB desirous of issuing Prepaid Payment Instruments (PPIs) shall
have its own ATM network and there should be no restrictions on acceptance or
repayment of deposits.
enriched_requirements.object: network and there should be no restrictions on acceptance or repayment of deposits

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Further, issuance of Prepaid Payment Instruments, shall
be subject to compliance with eligibility criteria and other guidelines as
prescribed by Department of Payment and Settlement Systems (DPSS), RBI
from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: with eligibility criteria and other guidelines as prescribed by Department of Payment and Settlement Systems (DPSS), RBI from
PPIs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) CRAR shall not be less than 10 percent in the current and preceding
financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: percent in the current and preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Gross NPAs shall be less than seven percent and net NPA shall not be
more than three percent in the current and preceding financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: percent in the current and preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Assessed net-worth shall be more than ₹25 crore as per the last
supervisory inspection;
enriched_requirements.object: crore as per the last supervisory inspection

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) An RCB shall not have defaulted in the maintenance of CRR/SLR during
the current and preceding financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: maintenance of CRR/SLR during the current and preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (6) An RCB shall have made a net profit in the preceding financial year;
enriched_requirements.object: profit in the preceding financial year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) An RCB shall have at least two professional directors on its Board and
shall have following systems and control in place:
enriched_requirements.object: professional directors on its Board and shall have following systems and control in place:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(9) An RCB shall not have been subjected to monetary penalty during last two
financial years and in the year of application;
enriched_requirements.object: monetary penalty during last two financial years and in the year of application

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(10) An RCB shall have satisfactorily implemented a comprehensive Board
approved policy on Customer grievance redressal mechanism which
includes escalation matrix for resolution of customer complaints.
enriched_requirements.object: comprehensive Board approved policy on Customer grievance redressal mechanism which includes escalation matrix for r

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The issuance and operation of prepaid instruments shall also be guided by the
instructions issued in this regard by DPSS, RBI from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: the instructions issued in this regard by DPSS, RBI from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these
Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
enriched_requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
enriched_requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
enriched_requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
<image>
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of
any provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
